modern_vehicle_description: A heavy-duty mining operations vehicle with the plasma war hammer in the cargo bed and the forest-fire ionizer built into the front plow. Officially it is a land-clearing vehicle for mineral extraction operations in the Dandaka region. Every meter of ground it crosses loses 60 years of underground ecosystem development.

same_animal: Hangul (Kashmir Stag) — but the bond is corrupted: his stag's antlers froze at six points and refuse to count any new year — the same animal locked in mid-thought
dark_animal_power: Mnemonic burning — reads a manuscript once with perfect recall then destroys the physical text — becomes the sole keeper of every text he destroys — anyone wishing to verify the past must apply to him and he edits as he recites
dark_bird_role: The Black-necked Crane refuses to land near him — his couriers are mechanical drone-cranes that mimic the bird but cannot cross the high passes — the bond replaced with surveillance
ancient_weapon_ability: An inkstone made of charred manuscript paper mixed with his own blood — what he writes with it becomes the historical record because the contradicting record no longer exists
modern_weapon_ability: A search-engine and content-moderation suppression algorithm — edits Wikipedia in real-time — rewrites cached web pages so the only surviving version is his — the modern manuscript-burning
dark_guru_name: His own perfect memory — he is his own corrupted guru with no living teacher
dark_guru_teaches: Coherence above truth — that contradiction is a disease and that purity of narrative is the cure — the ideal kingdom has one history and that one is his
dark_deity: Saraswati's shadow — the suppression of speech and the censorship of song
dark_deity_aspect: Sharada the Editor — the goddess of speech turned into the goddess of redaction
dark_deity_quality: Knowledge weaponised as exclusion — the swan that drowns the water it cannot drink
dark_vishnu_echo: Vamana inverted — the brahmin who takes the cosmos in three steps and gives nothing back
dark_shiva_form: None
dark_ganesha_inversion: Vighnakarta — the placer of obstacles not the remover — edits Wikipedia entries to dead-end on his version
mb_villain_quality: The brahmin teacher who loved his art so deeply that he taught only what served his pride — like Drona he must be defeated by a half-lie spoken in the name of truth — Sharad-Pandit claims the scroll is already uplinked before it is
mb_inversion_note: Drona's tragedy was that his loyalty was misplaced — Avantivarman's tragedy is that his loyalty is correctly placed in the Sharada-Pitha tradition but expressed as erasure rather than transmission
villain_motivation: Believes Kashmir's tragedy was not invasion but incoherence — too many gods too many languages too many contradicting histories — is curing the kingdom of its own confusion by editing the record to a single clean narrative — the pyre is his temple
hero_mirror_point: Both began as devoted students of the same Sharada-Pitha tradition — one copies twice and gives one away — the other reads once and burns the original — same love opposite custodianship
ancient_vehicle_description: A wheeled brazier-cart of black iron that carries a perpetual coal-pile — the cart's wheels leave a track of ash that does not melt in spring — the only thing he allows to follow him through the valley
modern_vehicle_name: Brahmacharya-Yantra Mk.II — The Mobile Server Farm
modern_vehicle_description: A black armoured server-truck with twelve internal hard-drive towers — each tower holds the only surviving copies of manuscripts he has destroyed — the truck is air-gapped and biometrically locked to him alone — the world's largest one-person library

same_animal: Saltwater Crocodile — but the bond is corrupted: his crocodile is the one that lived in the harbour drain beneath the Cellular Jail for forty years and ate prisoners' bones — the same animal that learned to wait for sentenced bodies
dark_animal_power: Locks any space — doors
dark_bird_role: ports
ancient_weapon_ability: even open water — wherever he places his hand isolation hardens and supplies cannot enter and ships cannot leave and signals die — turns geography itself into a prison
modern_weapon_name: The Nicobar Pigeon refuses to land on him or his ground — the bird's absence is the kingdom's distress signal
modern_weapon_ability: Sapta-Shala (Seven-Spoke Wheel)
dark_guru_name: A seven-spoked iron wheel modeled on the Cellular Jail's seven-wing radial design — wielded as a circular saw-shield that severs ropes comm cables and supply-lines — each spoke locks shut a different kind of exit
dark_guru_teaches: A signal-jamming biomimetic device built into a colonial-era brass meter — when activated it severs all radio communication within twenty kilometers and overlays the jammed frequencies with the recorded prisoner-screams from the jail's worst nights
dark_deity: His own remembered pain — the accumulated trauma of every prisoner who died in the Cellular Jail — there is no living teacher
dark_deity_aspect: none
dark_deity_quality: That isolation is the cure for incoherence — that the islands were designed for separation and any attempt at integration is the disease — that the British were the only empire that understood the islands correctly
dark_vishnu_echo: Varuna's shadow — the binder turned warder — the cosmic noose used to seal a prison rather than restore order
dark_shiva_form: Vrtra-of-the-Harbour — the serpent who blocks the rivers but here blocks the ports
dark_ganesha_inversion: The lock turned weapon — Varuna's noose used to hold men in cages rather than to enforce truth
mb_villain_parallel: Matsya inverted — the fish that drowns the survivor rather than carrying them to land
mb_villain_quality: None
mb_inversion_note: Vighnakarta — the placer of obstacles — every door he touches becomes harder to open
villain_motivation: Karna
hero_mirror_point: The warrior cursed by his own past — fighting for the wrong side because the world would not let him belong to the right one — must be addressed by his true name to be released
tags: Karna died fighting for Duryodhana — Kalapani-Asura serves the dead British administration because no other institution claimed him — must be released by name not by force
ancient_vehicle_name: Believes the islands were meant for isolation — the British designed the Andamans to be the place where troublesome bodies disappeared — and that purpose in his view is the islands' true vocation — the mainland is the disease
ancient_vehicle_description: Both have the same wound: an island used as a prison by every empire that ever ruled it — Onge-Nakshatra answers by opening doors and trade routes — Kalapani-Asura answers by hardening every lock the wound left behind
modern_vehicle_description: Sapta-Vahana (The Seven-Wing Convoy)
column33: A column of seven black-painted colonial-era armoured personnel carriers — modeled on the seven wings of the Cellular Jail — that drive in radial formation around any port they intend to seal — each vehicle carries one spoke of the Sapta-Shala wheel and one prisoner-record archive
column35: A column of seven communications-jammer trucks operating in radial formation — each truck broadcasts the prisoner-scream audio on a different frequency band — when activated simultaneously the islands fall silent on every channel including military VHF and satellite uplink
